mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show a positive correlation with FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: High real short-term interest rates combined with declining retail sales indicate a demand contraction under tight financial conditions, producing negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS declines and FEDFUNDS is elevated, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below -0.5 over 5-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects strengthening corporate output and earnings momentum, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during recovery phases, or the predictive IC drops below 0.02 in rolling 24-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling horizon, or Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in housing starts below 1.5 million units signals a collapse in construction demand and related supply chains, producing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1500.0 for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in > 70% of cases when HOUST stays below 1500.0 for two months, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices alongside slowing real GDP growth create stagflationary pressures that compress profit margins and trigger negative market repricing over the next month.
failure_condition: The CPIAUCSL-GDPC1 divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with a positive predictive accuracy below 45% across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in industrial production indicates immediate supply chain disruptions and earnings downgrades, causing negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances following a > 1.0% INDPRO decline, or the signal out-of-sample hit rate falls below 40%.
